name: Arctic Monkeys
bio: English rock band formed in Sheffield in 2002, blending witty, literate lyrics with garage and post-punk-influenced guitar sound. Their 2006 debut became the fastest-selling debut album in UK chart history.

name: Queens of the Stone Age
bio: American rock band formed by Josh Homme in 1996 following the breakup of Kyuss, fusing stoner rock roots with hard rock, alternative and psychedelic influences.

name: System of a Down
bio: Armenian-American alternative metal band formed in Glendale, California in 1994, known for politically charged lyrics and a genre-blending sound mixing metal, folk and pop influences.

name: Kent
bio: Swedish alternative rock band formed in Eskilstuna in 1990, one of Sweden's most commercially successful rock acts, singing almost exclusively in Swedish; disbanded in 2016 and briefly reunited for shows in 2025.

name: The Wombats
bio: English indie rock trio formed in Liverpool in 2003, known for witty, melancholic lyrics and hits like "Let's Dance to Joy Division" and "Greek Tragedy."

name: The Hives
bio: Swedish garage rock band formed in Fagersta in 1993, celebrated for high-energy live shows, matching stage suits, and hits like "Hate to Say I Told You So."

name: NOMARKMORE
bio: NOMARKMORE is a New York City-based alt-rock/alt-hip-hop project blending analog grind and digital noise into dark, cinematic songs, with rapid growth in 2025-2026 via releases like "Exhibition Number Zero."

name: Death From Above 1979
bio: Canadian dance-punk duo from Toronto formed in 2001 by Sebastien Grainger and Jesse Keeler, known for fusing punk aggression with dance grooves.

name: cleopatrick
bio: Canadian rock duo from Cobourg, Ontario, formed in 2015 by childhood friends Luke Gruntz and Ian Fraser, known for their raw guitar-and-drums sound.

name: Foo Fighters
bio: American rock band formed in Seattle in 1994 by former Nirvana drummer Dave Grohl, known for anthemic guitar-driven hits like "Everlong" and "Best of You."

name: Falling in Reverse
bio: American rock band formed in Las Vegas in 2008 by frontman Ronnie Radke, blending post-hardcore, metalcore and pop/electronic elements.

name: Weezer
bio: American rock band fronted by Rivers Cuomo, known for blending power pop with alternative/geek rock on albums like the "Blue Album" and Pinkerton.

name: Circa Waves
bio: English indie rock band led by singer-guitarist Kieran Shudall, formed in Liverpool in 2013.

name: Blink-182
bio: American pop punk band best known for "Enema of the State" and "Take Off Your Pants and Jacket," one of the genre's most commercially successful acts.

name: The Fume
bio: Swedish rock'n'roll trio from Gothenburg fronted by Phillip Harrt, playing glam-tinged garage punk influenced by Slade, David Bowie and Marc Bolan. The Hives personally picked them as support on a Scandinavian tour.

name: Des Rocs
bio: Solo project of American musician Danny Rocco, blending hard rock, glam rock and electro-rock influences; has toured with Muse and The Rolling Stones.

name: Royal Blood
bio: English rock duo of Mike Kerr (bass/vocals) and Ben Thatcher (drums); their 2014 self-titled debut was the UK's fastest-selling rock debut album in three years.

name: Highly Suspect
bio: American rock band fronted by Johnny Stevens; their debut album "Mister Asylum" and single "My Name Is Human" earned Grammy nominations.

name: Danko Jones
bio: Canadian hard rock trio fronted by namesake vocalist-guitarist Danko Jones, known for extensive touring and high-energy live shows.

name: NOFX
bio: American punk rock band fronted by bassist-vocalist Fat Mike, best known for the gold-certified 1994 album "Punk in Drublic."

name: Graveyard
genre: Hard rock, blues rock, psychedelic rock
bio: Swedish hard rock band from Gothenburg blending 1970s-style hard rock, blues and psychedelia; signed to Nuclear Blast Records.

name: Bloc Party
bio: English rock band co-founded by Kele Okereke and Russell Lissack; their acclaimed debut "Silent Alarm" (2005) established them as leaders of the mid-2000s post-punk revival.

name: Interpol
origin: New York City, New York, USA
bio: American rock band formed in Manhattan in 1997, known for blending post-punk atmospherics with driving, staccato rhythms.

name: Deaf Radio
bio: Guitar-driven alternative/stoner rock band from Athens, Greece; albums "Alarm" (2017) and "Modern Panic" (2019) won critical acclaim.

name: HIMALAYAS
bio: Welsh rock band from Cardiff signed to Nettwerk Records in 2022, releasing "From Hell To Here" (2023) and "Bad Star" (2025).

name: Eagles of Death Metal
bio: American rock band formed by Jesse Hughes and Josh Homme, known for a raw, glam-tinged garage-rock sound.

name: The Kooks
bio: English indie rock band fronted by Luke Pritchard; their multi-platinum debut "Inside In/Inside Out" (2006) made them a defining act of the mid-2000s UK indie scene.

name: The Killers
bio: American rock band fronted by Brandon Flowers, known for stadium anthems like "Mr. Brightside."

name: Phoenix
bio: French band formed in Versailles in 1995; won the Grammy for Best Alternative Music Album in 2010 for "Wolfgang Amadeus Phoenix."

name: Anna Ternheim
bio: Swedish singer-songwriter known for her introspective folk-pop sound; released her acclaimed debut "Somebody Outside" in 2004.

name: Jutes
bio: Stage name of Jordan Lutes, a Canadian musician blending alternative rock, pop-punk and hip-hop influences; Juno Award nominee for Breakthrough Artist of the Year (2026).

name: Arcade Fire
bio: Canadian indie rock band led by Win Butler and Régine Chassagne; "Funeral" (2004) and "The Suburbs" (2010, Grammy Album of the Year) made them hugely influential.

name: Father of Peace
bio: Israeli indie rock trio of Avshalom Ariel, May Alpert and Tom Bollig, who debuted in 2024 with the single "Escapism" followed by the album "The Year of Madness."

name: Nessa Barrett
bio: American singer-songwriter who first gained fame on TikTok before launching her music career with "Pain" in 2020.

name: The Weeknd
bio: Canadian singer, songwriter and producer (Abel Tesfaye) known for his dark, atmospheric R&B sound; one of the best-selling music artists in the world.

name: Green Day
bio: American punk rock trio (Billie Joe Armstrong, Mike Dirnt, Tré Cool) who broke through with 1994's "Dookie" and later the rock opera "American Idiot."

name: Millencolin
bio: Swedish skate-punk band that achieved international success with the gold-selling 2000 album "Pennybridge Pioneers."

name: Hov1
bio: Swedish hip-hop/pop group that became one of Sweden's most-streamed acts before disbanding in 2025 after a farewell tour.

name: Wolfmother
bio: Australian hard rock band centered on vocalist/guitarist Andrew Stockdale; their platinum 2005 debut spawned "Woman" and "Joker & the Thief."

name: Junior Brielle
bio: Swedish pop/indie-pop duo of brothers Gabriel and David Röhdin, acclaimed for the albums "Tampa" (2019) and "Domedagsdrömmar" (2023).

name: Billie Eilish
bio: American singer-songwriter who debuted with "Ocean Eyes" in 2015 and became a global star with "When We All Fall Asleep, Where Do We Go?"

name: Macklemore
bio: American rapper (Benjamin Haggerty) who achieved global success alongside producer Ryan Lewis with hits like "Thrift Shop" and "Can't Hold Us."

name: Linkin Park
bio: American rock band known for blending rock, hip-hop and electronic elements on albums like "Hybrid Theory" and "Meteora"; returned in 2024 with new vocalist Emily Armstrong.

name: The Thrills
bio: Irish indie-pop band best known for their platinum-selling 2003 debut "So Much for the City"; on indefinite hiatus since 2008.

name: Red Hot Chili Peppers
bio: American rock band known for blending funk, punk and psychedelic rock; inducted into the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame in 2012. A widely-circulated 2026 "farewell tour" (branded "One Last Ride"/"Bid the World Goodbye") comes entirely from aesplora.com and copycat SEO blogs - the same fabrication mill behind the fake Eminem farewell-tour rumor - and is contradicted by the band's official site and Ticketmaster/Live Nation, which list zero upcoming dates; last confirmed real show was Jul 23, 2023, Glasgow.

name: Cold War Kids
bio: American indie rock band known for its blues-rock sound, formed in Fullerton, California.

name: Editors
bio: English rock band fronted by Tom Smith, known for the singles "Munich" and "Blood" from their Mercury Prize-nominated debut "The Back Room."

name: Muse
bio: English rock trio (Matt Bellamy, Chris Wolstenholme, Dominic Howard) known for anthemic, genre-blending albums such as "Absolution" and "Black Holes and Revelations."

name: The Soundtrack of Our Lives
bio: Swedish rock band (T.S.O.O.L.) fronted by Ebbot Lundberg, drawing on 1960s/70s rock, punk and psychedelia; disbanded 2012, reunited 2023.

name: Death Cab for Cutie
bio: American rock band led by singer-songwriter Ben Gibbard, known for melodic, introspective indie rock such as the breakthrough album "Transatlanticism."

name: The Cardigans
bio: Swedish rock band fronted by Nina Persson, best known for the hit single "Lovefool" and albums like "First Band on the Moon" and "Gran Turismo."

name: Blur
bio: English rock band (Damon Albarn, Graham Coxon, Alex James, Dave Rowntree), central figures of the Britpop movement known for albums like "Parklife."

name: Oasis
bio: English rock band formed around brothers Liam and Noel Gallagher, regarded as the most globally successful group of the Britpop era.

name: Coldplay
bio: British rock band (Chris Martin, Jonny Buckland, Guy Berryman, Will Champion) celebrated for their live performances and albums like "Parachutes" and "Viva la Vida."

name: Louis Tomlinson
bio: English singer-songwriter who rose to fame in One Direction before launching his solo career in 2016. His albums include Walls, Faith in the Future, and How Did I Get Here?, and he is currently on his How Did We Get Here? world tour.

name: Harry Styles
bio: English singer-songwriter and actor who rose to fame in One Direction before a solo career blending pop, rock and glam influences across albums including Harry's House and Kiss All the Time, Disco, Occasionally. Currently on the Together, Together tour.

name: Zayn
bio: English singer-songwriter (Zayn Malik) who rose to fame in One Direction before launching a solo career in 2015 with an alternative R&B sound. His debut single Pillowtalk made him the first British male artist to debut at number one in both the UK and US simultaneously.

name: Thomas Stenström
bio: Karl Thomas Stenström is a Swedish pop singer-songwriter known for hits like Slå mig hårt i ansiktet and albums such as Superlativ 97 and Sverige, and has become one of Sweden's biggest live draws.

name: The Wannadies
bio: Swedish alternative rock/power pop band formed in Skellefteå in 1988, internationally known for You and Me Song (a UK Top 20 hit featured in Romeo + Juliet) and Might Be Stars. Split in 2003, reunited in 2016, and still touring.

name: Dropkick Murphys
bio: Irish-American Celtic punk band from the Boston area, blending traditional Irish folk instrumentation with hard-driving punk rock. Their 2005 song 'I'm Shipping Up to Boston' was featured in Martin Scorsese's The Departed and became their only platinum single.

name: Maxïmo Park
bio: English alternative rock band formed in Newcastle upon Tyne, known for sharply catchy, energetic guitar pop and frontman Paul Smith's flat cap. Debut album A Certain Trigger (2005) was Mercury Prize-nominated; the band celebrates its 20th anniversary in 2026 with the release of Stream of Life.

name: Franz Ferdinand
bio: Scottish rock band formed in Glasgow by Alex Kapranos, Nick McCarthy, Bob Hardy and Paul Thomson, known for angular, danceable post-punk revival hits like 'Take Me Out'. Their self-titled 2004 debut won the Mercury Prize and was nominated for a Grammy.

name: Hard-Fi
bio: English indie rock band from Staines-upon-Thames whose 2005 debut Stars of CCTV, recorded in a converted mini-cab office, produced hits 'Hard to Beat', 'Cash Machine' and 'Living for the Weekend' and became a fixture of mid-2000s indie discos.

name: OutKast
bio: Atlanta hip-hop duo of Big Boi and André 3000, widely considered one of the most influential rap acts ever, blending funk, psychedelia and jazz into Southern hip hop. Speakerboxxx/The Love Below (2003) won Album of the Year at the Grammys. No touring activity is currently scheduled; this entry will pick up any future reunion shows automatically.

name: Black Eyed Peas
bio: Los Angeles group built around will.i.am, apl.de.ap and Taboo (with Fergie fronting the group through their 2000s commercial peak). Broke through with Elephunk (2003) and became global pop-rap hitmakers with anthems like 'I Gotta Feeling' and 'Boom Boom Pow'. Their current European stadium/festival run (June-July 2026) has already wrapped by the time this was researched; no further confirmed dates yet.

name: JAY-Z
bio: Born Shawn Corey Carter in Brooklyn's Marcy Houses, JAY-Z is one of the best-selling and most Grammy-awarded rappers ever, and co-founder/chairman of Roc Nation. His 2026 shows mark the 30th anniversary of his debut album Reasonable Doubt and 25th of The Blueprint.

name: Eminem
bio: Born Marshall Bruce Mathers III, Eminem broke through with The Slim Shady LP (1999) after signing to Dr. Dre's Aftermath Entertainment, becoming one of the best-selling and most awarded rappers ever. Note: a widely-circulated "One Last Ride" 2026 farewell tour is an unconfirmed rumor from low-reliability sites; Ticketmaster and his official site show no announced tour dates as of this research, so none were added.

name: Metallica
bio: Formed by James Hetfield and Lars Ulrich, Metallica are pioneers of thrash metal and one of the best-selling bands in history. Master of Puppets (1986) and the self-titled 'Black Album' (1991) are landmark releases. Currently running an extended Sphere residency in Las Vegas as part of the M72 World Tour.

name: Mew
bio: Danish alternative/prog-leaning indie band led by Jonas Bjerre, known for atmospheric, falsetto-driven 'indie stadium' rock. Breakthrough came with Frengers (2003); No More Stories... (2009) and +- (2015) both hit No. 1 in Denmark. Their most recently confirmed shows (Helsinki Jan 30 and London Feb 5, 2026) have already passed; no further dates confirmed yet.

name: Maskinen
bio: Swedish rap/electro duo of Herbert "Afasi" Munkhammar and Frej Larsson (also of Slagsmålsklubben), known for festival anthems like 'Segertåget' and 'Alla Som Inte Dansar'. Active 2007-2015, then returned in 2024 after a nine-year break. No upcoming shows currently confirmed (spelning.se lists 0 upcoming dates as of this research).

name: Mumford & Sons
bio: English folk-rock band led by Marcus Mumford, blending banjo, mandolin and stomping choruses. Broke through with Sigh No More (2009) and won the Album of the Year Grammy for Babel (2012). Currently touring North America in support of new album Prizefighter.

name: Grandaddy
bio: Led by Jason Lytle, Grandaddy blend lo-fi indie rock with synths and melancholy tales of technology and suburban California life. Their 2000 album The Sophtware Slump is a widely cited indie-rock landmark; most of their 2026 US tour dates (a Sophtware Slump anniversary run) have already passed, with one date still ahead.

name: The Shins
bio: James Mercer's long-running indie rock project, prized for wistful psych-pop melodies (Oh, Inverted World, 2001; Wincing the Night Away, 2007) and the Garden State soundtrack. No verified upcoming shows found; a Songkick listing under their name turned out to actually be Weezer's arena tour (data mixup), so it was not used.

name: Brainpool
bio: Swedish power-pop band from Lund formed by Jan "Janne" Kask, David Birde, Christoffer Lundquist and Jens Jansson. Big in Sweden in the mid-90s with Soda (1994), Painkiller (1995) and Stay Free (1996); continued as a trio after Kask left in 1997, later releasing the rock opera Junk (2004). No upcoming shows currently scheduled.

name: Kashmir
bio: Danish alternative rock band led by Kasper Eistrup, one of Denmark's biggest rock exports; No Balance Palace (2005) featured guest turns from David Bowie and Lou Reed. Went on indefinite hiatus in 2017 with occasional reunion activity since. Note: a same-named US Led Zeppelin tribute act dominates ticketing-site search results under "Kashmir" — those tour dates are NOT this band and were not added.

name: Razorlight
bio: English indie rock band fronted by Johnny Borrell, known for the UK No.1 single 'America' from their chart-topping 2006 self-titled album. Reunited classic lineup (Borrell, Ågren, Dalemo, Burrows) touring in 2026 for the album's 20th anniversary.

name: Shout Out Louds
bio: Stockholm indie-pop band led by Adam Olenius, formed by childhood friends. Broke through with debut Howl Howl Gaff Gaff (2003), later releasing Our Ill Wills (2007) and Optica (2013). Still active and touring in support of their back catalogue.

name: Band of Horses
bio: American indie rock/Americana band formed by Ben Bridwell, known for sweeping, anthemic songs like 'The Funeral' and 'No One's Gonna Love You'. Infinite Arms (2010) earned a Grammy nomination. Currently on an extensive world tour through late 2026.

name: The Coral
bio: English band from the Wirral blending 60s psychedelia, folk-rock and pop, led by James Skelly. Their Mercury-nominated 2002 self-titled debut and singles like 'Dreaming of You' made them a fixture of the UK indie scene.

name: The Strokes
bio: New York band led by Julian Casablancas, credited with kickstarting the early-2000s garage rock revival with their 2001 debut Is This It. Touring in 2026 in support of new album Reality Awaits.

name: Pharrell Williams
bio: Virginia Beach-born producer, songwriter and performer, half of The Neptunes production duo with Chad Hugo and frontman of N.E.R.D. One of the most influential pop/R&B producers of the 2000s ('Happy', Daft Punk's 'Get Lucky'). His own festival, Something in the Water, has no confirmed 2026 dates after its Virginia Beach agreement was terminated; no reliable solo tour dates found.

name: Mando Diao
bio: Swedish alternative rock band formed by Björn Dixgård and Gustaf Norén in Borlänge in the mid-1990s. Broke through with the album Hurricane Bar (2004) and hit "Long Before Rock'n'Roll". Main fanbase in Sweden, Germany, Austria, Switzerland and Japan.

name: Johnossi
bio: Swedish indie rock duo formed in Stockholm in 2004 by singer-guitarist John Engelbert and drummer Oskar "Ossi" Bonde (name = John + Ossi). Fierce, garage-influenced rock in the vein of The Black Keys/The White Stripes; albums include All They Ever Wanted (2008), Mad Gone Wild (2022) and Forevers (2024). No upcoming tour dates confirmed as of this research.

name: Friska Viljor
bio: Swedish indie-pop/rock duo, Daniel Johansson and Joakim Sveningsson, founded in Stockholm in January 2005. Debut album Bravo! (2006/2007) launched an international career; eight albums released over the years. A June 2026 German club tour (Cologne, Hamburg, Berlin, Munich) is already past relative to current date, and a confirmed Reeperbahn Festival (Hamburg, Sep 16-19 2026) appearance has no specific day announced, so no concerts were added.

name: Kaizers Orchestra
bio: Norwegian theatrical alternative rock band formed in Bergen on 1 January 2000, singing in Norwegian and blending rock, punk and Eastern-European/Balkan-influenced folk ("Balajka"). Broke through with "Ompa til du dor", followed by Evig Pint, Maestro and the Violeta Violeta trilogy. Split after a farewell tour in 2013, reunited in 2022 and sold out 56 comeback shows in 15 minutes. Currently on a 25th-anniversary European club tour.

name: The Sounds
bio: Swedish new wave/indie rock band formed in Helsingborg in 1998 by Maja Ivarsson, Felix Rodriguez, Johan Bengtsson and Jesper Anderberg, drawing comparisons to Blondie and The Cars. Known internationally for "Living in America" and albums like Dying to Say This to You. One confirmed 2026 date found so far: Darker Waves festival, Huntington Beach, California, Nov 14, 2026 (US, too far for distance to be practically relevant but added as verified).

name: Damien Rice
bio: Irish singer-songwriter (b. 1973, Dublin) known for stark, confessional folk-rock. Cut his teeth in the band Juniper (1991-1998) before going solo; debut album O (2002) launched his solo career, followed by 9 and My Favourite Faded Fantasy. No upcoming tour dates confirmed as of this research.

name: MGMT
bio: American psychedelic pop duo Andrew VanWyngarden and Benjamin Goldwasser, formed in Middletown, Connecticut in 2002 (originally as "The Management"). Breakthrough album Oracular Spectacular (2007) produced global hits "Time to Pretend", "Electric Feel" and "Kids". No confirmed upcoming tour dates found as of this research.

name: Snoop Dogg
bio: Calvin Cordozar Broadus Jr. (b. 1971), pioneering West Coast rapper and G-funk figurehead since Doggystyle (1993). A widely-circulated "Eminem/Snoop Dogg/50 Cent 2026 tour" from low-reliability aggregator sites (toursetlist.com) looks unverified/exaggerated and was not used. One officially confirmed date found: Hard Rock Live at Etess Arena, Atlantic City, Aug 24, 2026 (Ticketmaster-listed). An earlier Prague O2 Arena date (Mar 10, 2026) had already passed relative to current date.

name: Fu Manchu
bio: American stoner rock band formed in Orange County, California in 1985 (originally the hardcore band Virulence), fronted by Scott Hill. Thirteenth studio album The Return of Tomorrow released June 2024. On a summer 2026 European tour; several festival appearances (Rockstadt Extreme Fest, Blue Moon Festival, Rock The Lakes) had no single confirmed performance day and were excluded, only clear single-date club shows were added.

name: Mustasch
bio: Swedish hard rock band from Gothenburg formed in 1998 by Ralf Gyllenhammar, Hannes Hansson, Mats Hansson and Stam Johansson; name inspired by mustachioed heroes like Freddie Mercury. Released new music and toured Sweden/Finland in spring 2026 (Malmo Mar 6, other dates Apr 10 and Jul 7) - all already past relative to current date, no dates confirmed beyond that as of this research.

name: Gogol Bordello
bio: Gypsy punk band formed in New York City in 1999 by frontman Eugene Hütz, fusing punk rock with Romani and Eastern European folk, ska and cabaret. New album We Mean It, Man! released February 2026. A North American tour in Feb-March 2026 has already passed; a Fall 2026 European tour (starting Frankfurt Sep 26) is upcoming.

name: The Ark
bio: Swedish glam rock band formed by Ola Salo near Vaxjo in 1991; disbanded 2011, reunited in 2020. One of Scandinavia's most successful rock exports of the 2000s ("It Takes a Fool to Remain Sane", "One of Us Is Gonna Die Young"). No confirmed 2026 tour dates found as of this research.

name: Riddarna
bio: Swedish rock band from Gotland formed in 2009 (Edward Forslund, Erik Dahlstrom, Patrik Jakubowski Arnold); minimalistic, intense rock. Fifth album Utomjordingar released March 2026 to strong reviews, followed by a spring club tour (Stockholm, Gothenburg, Malmo - all now past).

name: The Datsuns
bio: New Zealand garage/hard rock band formed in Cambridge in 1998 (Dolf de Borst, Christian Livingstone, Phil Somervell), raw and Stooges/Deep Purple-influenced. Self-titled 2002 debut hit #1 in NZ and UK top 20; toured supporting Metallica, The White Stripes and Black Sabbath. No specific confirmed 2026 tour dates found beyond general New Zealand/Australia plans as of this research.

name: The White Stripes
bio: American garage/blues-rock duo Jack White and Meg White, formed in Detroit in 1997; leaders of the early-2000s garage rock revival, disbanded in 2011. Widely-circulated 2026 "reunion tour" claims (new album, world tour) are unconfirmed rumor stemming from Third Man Records archival vinyl/vault reissues - no official reunion or tour dates from Jack White, Meg White, or Third Man Records as of this research, so none were added.

name: Kyuss
bio: Pioneering American stoner rock band formed in Palm Desert, California in 1987, known for down-tuned, psychedelic desert rock; disbanded 1995. Alumni went on to Queens of the Stone Age, Fu Manchu, Mondo Generator and others. A 2010-2012 "Kyuss Lives!" reunion (without Josh Homme) was legally forced to rebrand as Vista Chino; no active band or 2026 tour dates exist as of this research.

name: Marcy Playground
bio: American alternative rock trio (John Wozniak, Dylan Keefe, Shlomi Lavie) named after a Minneapolis school; best known for the 1997 #1 Modern Rock hit "Sex and Candy". A Songkick listing under their name for summer 2026 turned out to actually show Everclear tour cards (Minnesota Yacht Club Festival, Boxcar Park Everett) - a data mixup similar to prior Shins/Weezer and Kashmir cases - so it was not used; no reliable Marcy Playground-specific dates were added.

name: Papa Roach
genre: Alternative Metal / Nu Metal / Rap Rock
bio: American rock band from Vacaville, California, formed 1993, defining nu-metal sound of the early 2000s with singer Jacoby Shaddix. Best known for "Last Resort"; celebrated the 25th anniversary of breakthrough album Infest in 2025. On the "Rise of the Roach" world tour, with a UK/Ireland arena run in November 2026.

name: Bloodhound Gang
origin: King of Prussia, Pennsylvania, United States
bio: American satirical alternative rock band formed in King of Prussia, Pennsylvania in 1992 by Jimmy Pop and Evil Jared Hasselhoff, mixing punk, hip-hop and europop with deliberately crude, comedic lyrics ("The Bad Touch", "Fire Water Burn"). No confirmed upcoming tour dates found as of this research.

name: Jimmy Eat World
bio: American rock band from Mesa, Arizona formed in 1993 (Jim Adkins, Tom Linton, Rick Burch, Zach Lind), key act of the early-2000s emo/pop-punk boom. Breakthrough album Bleed American (2001) produced "The Middle". Touring North America, UK and Australia in 2026 for the album's 25th anniversary.

name: The Offspring
bio: American punk rock band formed in Garden Grove, California in 1984 (Dexter Holland, Noodles Wasserman), central to the mid-90s mainstream punk revival alongside Green Day and Bad Religion; over 40 million records sold. On the "Supercharged Worldwide in 26" tour; a January-February 2026 North American arena run with Bad Religion has already passed. Several October/November 2026 festival slots (Sea.Hear.Now, Aftershock, Mission Bayfest, Corona Capital) had no confirmed single performance day and were excluded.

name: The Hoosiers
origin: Yatton, Somerset, England (band name from Indianapolis, Indiana)
bio: English pop rock duo Irwin Sparkes and Alan Sharland; broke through in 2007 with "Goodbye Mr A" and "Worried About Ray". Sixth album Compassion released May 2026, followed by a full UK headline tour in October 2026.

name: Kasabian
bio: English rock band formed in Leicester in 1997, led by Sergio Pizzorno (and formerly Tom Meighan); neo-psych/electronica-indie fusion compared to Primal Scream and Oasis. Four UK #1 albums; NME Best Live Act and Brit Award winners. Played a huge Finsbury Park London headline show and several European festivals in mid-2026; the Naples and Leeds Festival dates remain upcoming.

name: Rage Against the Machine
bio: American rap-metal band formed in Los Angeles in 1991 (Zack de la Rocha, Tom Morello, Tim Commerford, Brad Wilk), known for anti-authoritarian, revolutionary lyrics fused with heavy funk-metal riffs. A planned 2026 reunion tour with festival headline slots was put on hold after Zack de la Rocha suffered a serious Achilles tendon injury; no active tour dates as of this research.

name: Royal Republic
bio: Swedish rock band formed in Malmo in late 2007 (Adam Grahn, Hannes Irengard, Jonas Almen, Per Andreasson), energetic guitar rock in the vein of Mando Diao and The Hives. Debut We Are The Royal (2010) featured "Tommy Gun". Fifth album LoveCop recently released; touring Germany, France, Poland and Spain through 2026.

name: Timbuktu
bio: Jason Michael Bosak Diakite (b. 1975, Lund), Swedish rapper/singer performing as Timbuktu; influential political artist drawing on folk, blues, West African and Jamaican music. Bilingual (Swedish/English), son of American parents. Homecoming show at Malmo Arena on Nov 21, 2026 with funk band Damn! - the biggest performance of his career, close to his native Skane.
